_comment: Synthetic corpus for the running-header/footer (boilerplate) detection benchmark (scripts/benchmark_boilerplate.py). Each scenario is rendered to a real multi-page PDF in memory with PyMuPDF, with known headers/footers/page-numbers injected at known positions. Because the boilerplate is injected, per-block ground-truth labels are exact, so the benchmark can report true precision/recall/F1 with no network and no manual labeling. Scenarios are ordered roughly by the refinement they stress: simple repetition, digit-varying page numbers, odd/even (recto/verso) alternation, multi-line header blocks, section-scoped headers, plus two precision guardrails (a clean document and a number-heavy body) that must NOT be stripped.

scenarios.name: simple
scenarios.stresses: Constant header + footer with a varying page number.

scenarios.name: page_number_only
scenarios.stresses: Bare page number in the footer; only detectable after digit normalization.

scenarios.name: odd_even
scenarios.stresses: Recto/verso alternating running headers; each appears on only ~50% of pages.

scenarios.name: multiline
scenarios.stresses: Two-line header rendered as a single block.

scenarios.name: section_scoped
scenarios.stresses: Running header changes every section; no single header reaches a document-wide frequency.

scenarios.name: clean_control
scenarios.stresses: Precision guardrail: no boilerplate at all. The detector must strip nothing.

scenarios.name: numeric_body
scenarios.stresses: Precision guardrail: short numeric lines in the body (table refs, equations, figures) that a naive digit/short-line filter would wrongly delete.
